revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ceb:Hirosaki Kōen]][[ja:弘前公園]][[ko:히로사키 공원]][[zh:弘前公園]]
'''Hirosaki Park''' (弘前公園) is a {{ill|Park|en|Park|qid=Q22698|lt=park|1=Park}} located in {{ill|Hirosaki|en|Hirosaki|qid=Q194087|lt=Hirosaki|1=Hirosaki}}, {{ill|Aomori Prefecture|en|Aomori Prefecture|qid=Q71699|lt=Aomori Prefecture|1=Aomori Prefecture}}, Japan. It is also known as '''Ōyōkōen''' (鷹揚公園) or '''Ōyōen''' (鷹揚園). According to the Hirosaki City Urban Park Ordinance, its official name is "Ōyōen"<ref>[http://www3.e-reikinet.jp/hirosaki/d1w_reiki/41890101015200000000/41890101015200000000/41890101015200000000.html 弘前市都市公園条例]</ref>.
The park is renowned as one of the most famous cherry blossom viewing spots in Japan, featuring around 50 varieties and 2,600 cherry trees that bloom in spring.
Hirosaki Park and Ōyōkōen have been selected as one of the {{ill|100 Notable Urban Parks in Japan|ja|日本の都市公園100選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Notable Urban Parks in Japan|qq=}}, {{ill|100 Notable Historical Parks in Japan|ja|日本の歴史公園100選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Notable Historical Parks in Japan|qq=}}, and {{ill|100 Famous Cherry Blossom Spots in Japan|ja|日本さくら名所100選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Famous Cherry Blossom Spots in Japan|qq=}}. Inside the park, {{ill|Hirosaki Castle|en|Hirosaki Castle|qid=Q328582|lt=Hirosaki Castle|1=Hirosaki Castle}} is designated as one of the {{ill|100 Fine Castles of Japan|en|100 Fine Castles of Japan|qid=Q1139439|lt=100 Fine Castles of Japan|1=100 Fine Castles of Japan}}, part of the {{ill|100 Historical Landscapes of Japan|ja|美しい日本の歴史的風土100選|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/100 Historical Landscapes of Japan|qq=}}, and included in the {{ill|100 Landscapes of Japan (Heisei era)|en|100 Landscapes of Japan (Heisei era)|qid=Q2806720|lt=Heisei Sceneries|1=100 Landscapes of Japan (Heisei era)}}.
== Overview == * Location: 1 Shimoshirogane-cho, Hirosaki City, Aomori Prefecture * Area: Approximately50 hectares * Major festivals held in the park: ** {{ill|Hirosaki Sakura Festival|ja|弘前さくらまつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirosaki Sakura Festival|1=Hirosaki Cherry Blossom Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11487199}} (Late April to early May) ** {{ill|Hirosaki Chrysanthemum and Autumn Foliage Festival|ja|弘前城菊と紅葉まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirosaki Chrysanthemum and Autumn Foliage Festival|1=Hirosaki Castle Chrysanthemum and Autumn Leaves Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11487216}} (Mid-October to early November) ** {{ill|Hirosaki Castle Snow Lantern Festival|ja|弘前城雪燈籠まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirosaki Castle Snow Lantern Festival|1=Hirosaki Castle Snow Lantern Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11487218}} (Mid-February)
== History == * {{ill|1894|en|1894|qid=Q7843|lt=1894|1=1894}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 27): {{ill|Tsugaru clan|en|Tsugaru clan|qid=Q1062437|lt=Tsugaru Clan|1=Tsugaru clan}} leased the neglected Hirosaki Castle after the {{ill|Abolition of the han system|en|Abolition of the han system|qid=Q331905|lt=Abolition of the han system|1=Abolition of the han system}}. * {{ill|1895|en|1895|qid=Q7844|lt=1895|1=1895}} (Meiji 28): Opened to the public as "Hirosaki Park." * {{ill|1902|en|1902|qid=Q2043|lt=1902|1=1902}} (Meiji 35): Taken over by Hirosaki City. * {{ill|1908|en|1908|qid=Q2056|lt=1908|1=1908}} (Meiji 41): {{ill|Emperor Taishō|en|Emperor Taishō|qid=Q157052|lt=Taisho Emperor|1=Emperor Taishō}}, then Crown Prince, visited the park and named it "Ōyōen." * {{ill|1918|en|1918|qid=Q2094|lt=1918|1=1918}} ({{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|qid=Q329174|lt=Taisho|1=Taishō era}} 7): The first cherry blossom viewing event was held. * {{ill|1945|en|1945|qid=Q5240|lt=1945|1=1945}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Showa|1=Shōwa era}} 19): A new bridge, {{ill|Ichiyōbashi|ja|一陽橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichiyōbashi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was constructed at the Kamekōchō side. * {{ill|1962|en|1962|qid=Q2764|lt=1962|1=1962}} (Showa 37): The first Chrysanthemum and Autumn Foliage Festival was held. * {{ill|1977|en|1977|qid=Q2481|lt=1977|1=1977}} (Showa 52): The Snow Lantern Festival was initiated. * {{ill|1988|en|1988|qid=Q2426|lt=1988|1=1988}} (Showa 63): A botanical garden opened in the San-no-maru area. * {{ill|1989|en|1989|qid=Q2425|lt=1989|1=1989}} ({{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 1): During the Sakura Festival period, admission to the Honmaru, Kita-no-kuruwa, and Ninomaru areas became paid for the first time. * {{ill|1995|en|1995|qid=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}} (Heisei 7): The park celebrated its 100th anniversary. * {{ill|2003|en|2003|qid=Q1986|lt=2003|1=2003}} (Heisei 15): Adjustments were made to admission periods and areas. * {{ill|2020|en|2020|qid=Q25337|lt=2020|1=2020}} ({{ill|Reiwa era|en|Reiwa era|qid=Q62609017|lt=Reiwa|1=Reiwa era}} 2): The 100th Hirosaki Sakura Festival was canceled due to the {{ill|SARS-CoV-2|en|SARS-CoV-2|qid=Q82069695|lt=COVID-19 pandemic|1=SARS-CoV-2}}. The park was closed from April 10 to May 17.<ref>[http://www.mutusinpou.co.jp/news/2020/04/59342.html 【速報】弘前公園を10日から閉鎖／コロナ感染防止で] - 陸奥新報・2020th year 4月1日</ref>.
== Paid Admission Areas == Residents of the city who are elementary or junior high school students, or 65 years old and above, are admitted for free. Group discounts (for 10 or more people) and combination tickets for Honmaru/Kita-no-kuruwa and the Botanical Garden are also available. Details are available on the Hirosaki City website.
* Honmaru and Kita-no-kuruwa: ** Open from April 1 to November 23 annually. Hours are from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM (7:00 AM to 9:00 PM during the Sakura Festival). Free admission is available on the last Sunday of May.<ref>Implemented since 2008.</ref> Admission includes access to the Hirosaki Castle Historical Museum. * {{ill|Hirosaki Castle Botanical Garden|ja|弘前城植物園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirosaki Castle Botanical Garden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135578992|1=Hirosaki Castle Botanical Garden}}: ** Open from mid-April to November 23. Hours are from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM.
== Landmarks and Facilities == {{Wide image|Hirosaki castle nishibori2.JPG|530px|West Moat of Hirosaki Park}} * {{ill|Hirosaki Castle|en|Hirosaki Castle|qid=Q328582|lt=Hirosaki Castle|1=Hirosaki Castle}} ({{ill|Tenshu|ja|Tenshu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) ** Hirosaki Castle Historical Museum (Inside the castle tower) * {{ill|Castle gates|ja|城門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Castle gates|1=City gate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q82117}} (Not open to the public): North Gate, East Gate, East Inner Gate, South Inner Gate, Otemon Gate * {{ill|Yagura (castle)|ja|櫓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagura (castle)|1=yagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11544657}} (Not open to the public): Tatsumi Yagura, Minami Yagura, Ushitora Yagura * {{ill|Aomori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|ja|青森県護国神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomori Prefecture Gokoku Shrine|1=Aomori Gokoku Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q21652327}} * {{ill|Hirosaki City Museum|en|Hirosaki City Museum|qid=Q28154389|lt=Hirosaki City Museum|1=Hirosaki City Museum}} * {{ill|Hirosaki Civic Hall|ja|弘前市民会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirosaki Civic Hall|1=Hirosaki Civic Hall|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11487234}} * Botanical Garden * Green Consultation Office * Nishi Moat Boat Rentals * Nakabori Sightseeing Boats: Operates during festivals.<ref name="tn1905">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.mutusinpou.co.jp/news/2019/05/55462.html|title=弘前公園「観光舟」運行拡大へ船頭養成|publisher=[[Tōō Ni11o]]|date=2019-05-14|accessdate=2020-10-03}}</ref> Local boatmen began operating in 2019.<ref name="tn1905"/><ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.shimotsuke.co.jp/articles/-/173633|title=地元の船頭がもてなしを 栃木のNPO法人が弘前で育成中、ねぷた祭りでデビューへ|publisher=[[Shimotsuke Shimbun]]|date=2019-05-24|accessdate=2020-10-03}}</ref>. * Recreational spaces: Recreation Square, Picnic Square, Civic Plaza * Shops and dining: Takekoden, Hirosaki City Product Center, Kuma no Mise * Restaurant: Yamazen
== Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Tsugaru Neputa Village|ja|津軽藩ねぷた村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsugaru Neputa Village|1=Tsugaru-han Neputa Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11556435}} * {{ill|Former Main Branch of the 59th National Bank|ja|旧第五十九銀行本店本館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Main Branch of the 59th National Bank|1=Former Main Branch of the 59th National Bank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11511115}} (Aomori Bank Memorial Hall) * {{ill|Otemon Plaza|ja|追手門広場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otemon Plaza|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ** Hirosaki Tourism Hall and Floats Exhibition Hall ** Hirosaki Literature Museum ** {{ill|Hirosaki City Library|ja|弘前市立図書館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hirosaki City Library|1=Hirosaki City Public Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q60988531}} ** {{ill|Former Hirosaki City Library|ja|旧弘前市立図書館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Hirosaki City Library|1=Former Hirosaki City Library|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11511055}} ** {{ill|Former Tōō Gijuku Foreign Teacher’s House|ja|旧東奥義塾外人教師館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Former Tōō Gijuku Foreign Teacher’s House|1=Former Tōō Gijuku Missionary House|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11511074}} ** Miniature Architecture Models * {{ill|Fujita Memorial Garden|ja|藤田記念庭園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujita Memorial Garden|qq=}} * {{ill|Chōshō-ji Temple|ja|長勝寺 (弘前市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshō-ji Temple|1=Chōshō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11651542}} * Hirosaki City Hall * Hirosaki Cultural Center * {{ill|Aomori Prefectural Hirosaki Chuo High School|ja|青森県立弘前中央高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomori Prefectural Hirosaki Chuo High School|1=Aomori Prefectural Hirosaki Chuo High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11662243}} * {{ill|Aomori Prefectural Hirosaki Technical High School|ja|青森県立弘前工業高等学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aomori Prefectural Hirosaki Technical High School|1=Aomori Prefectural Hirosaki Technical High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11662250}}
== Access == === By Bus === * {{ill|Kōnan Bus Company|en|Kōnan Bus Company|qid=Q6455226|lt=Kōnan Bus|1=Kōnan Bus Company}} ** From {{ill|Hirosaki Station|en|Hirosaki Station|qid=Q5770722|lt=Hirosaki Station|1=Hirosaki Station}} (JR and Kōnan Railway), take the "Dotemachi 100 Yen Circular Bus" or other lines. Alight at City Hall or other nearby stops.
=== By Car === * Approximately30 minutes from {{ill|Owani Hirosaki Interchange|ja|大鰐弘前インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owani Hirosaki Interchange|1=Owani-Hirosaki interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10938610}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Expressway|en|Tōhoku Expressway|qid=Q428428|lt=Tohoku Expressway|1=Tōhoku Expressway}}.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Tourist attractions in Aomori Prefecture|ja|青森県の観光地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tourist attractions in Aomori Prefecture|1=Visitor attractions in Aomori prefecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11662165}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Hirosaki Park}} * [https://www.hirosakipark.jp/ Official Website - Hirosaki Park] * [https://www.hirosaki-kanko.or.jp/web/edit.html?id=cat01_hirosakipark Hirosaki Park Area - Hirosaki Tourism and Convention Association] * [http://www.hirosakipark.or.jp/hirosakipark/ Hirosaki Park (Hirosaki Castle Museum)] * [https://www.city.hirosaki.aomori.jp/hakubutsukan/ Hirosaki City Museum] * [http://www.hirosakipark.or.jp/plant/index.html Hirosaki Castle Botanical Garden] * [http://www.hirosakipark.or.jp/green/index.html Hirosaki City Green Consultation Office] * [https://www.city.hirosaki.aomori.jp/gaiyou/shisetsu/2015-0223-1057-41.html Otemon Plaza - Hirosaki Tourism Hall] * [https://www.city.hirosaki.aomori.jp/tosho/ Hirosaki City Library] * [https://www.city.hirosaki.aomori.jp/hirosakibunka/ Hirosaki Cultural Center] * [https://www.city.hirosaki.aomori.jp/gaiyou/livecam/ Live Camera - Hi-it! (Hirosaki Lifelong Learning Network System)] {{translated page|ja|弘前公園|version=104474084|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:弘前公園]]
